title: Equal Protection
orientation: A pillar page laying out the abolitionist case that abortion must be criminalized as murder under equal protection of the law, including for mothers who procure abortions. Aimed at pro-life Christians, legislators, and movement-curious readers weighing whether automatic immunity for mothers is just.
audiences: Pro-life Christian reconsidering automatic immunity for mothers
State legislator weighing an equal protection bill
Skeptic asking how abolitionists differ from the pro-life establishment
Post-Roe policy analyst studying self-managed abortion

questions.question: Why do abolitionists insist that mothers must also be subject to prosecution for abortion?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian reconsidering automatic immunity for mothers
questions.answer: Abolitionists argue that equal protection of the law requires that anyone who knowingly participates in killing a preborn child, including the mother, can face the same penalties available for murdering any other person. Carving the mother out creates a class of people uniquely shielded, which violates the impartiality the Bible commands of judges. Mothers would still receive due process and the presumption of innocence like every other defendant.
questions.quote: It would mean that nobody — not father, mother, doctor, or anyone else — is permitted to participate in the murder of a child in the womb. It would mean the available penalties for murdering any other person would be available for anyone found guilty of knowingly murdering a preborn human being.

questions.question: How does the article ground equal protection in Scripture rather than just the Constitution?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian reconsidering automatic immunity for mothers
questions.answer: The piece treats equal justice as the American legal expression of the biblical principle of impartiality, citing Leviticus 19:15's command to Israel's judges. On this view, partiality in the law toward mothers who abort is not merely bad policy but a violation of God's standard for judges.
questions.quote: Put simply, equal justice is the American legal term for the Biblical principle of impartiality. Leviticus 19:15 commands to Israel's judges, "You shall do no injustice in court. You shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great, but in righteousness shall you judge your neighbor." Equal justice is the fulfillment of this principle.

questions.question: What does the 14th Amendment have to do with abolishing abortion?
questions.audience: State legislator weighing an equal protection bill
questions.answer: The article reads the 14th Amendment's equal protection clause as forbidding states from creating classes of people who get different levels of legal protection. Properly applied, it both prohibits homicide against preborn human beings and prohibits states from treating them differently than the born under law.
questions.quote: Properly understood and applied, the 14th Amendment prohibits any state from allowing crimes like homicide to be committed against preborn human beings, and further prohibits states from treating preborn children differently under law.

questions.question: What happened with the Abolition of Abortion in Louisiana Act in 2022?
questions.answer: The bill cleared the Louisiana House Criminal Justice Committee 7-2 with unanimous Republican support. Before the floor vote, 76 pro-life organizations led by National Right to Life sent a letter to legislators nationwide urging them to oppose any bill that allowed prosecution of mothers, and nearly all the previously supportive Republicans flipped, defeating the bill.
questions.quote: In April 2022, the Abolition of Abortion in Louisiana Act passed through the Louisiana House Criminal Justice Committee by a 7-2 vote. Every Republican supported the measure. But on the day of the floor vote, 76 pro-life organizations led by National Right to Life, published a letter that was sent to every state legislator in the country, asking pro-life legislators to oppose any bill that allows prosecution for mothers who murder their children:

questions.question: How does giving mothers immunity effectively legalize self-managed abortion?
questions.audience: Post-Roe policy analyst studying self-managed abortion
questions.answer: If a mother can never be prosecuted, then any abortion she accomplishes without the help of a third party is functionally legal. The article argues this is exactly the regulatory environment the abortion industry is racing toward, with mail-order pills, hotlines, and training organizations already operating in the United States. Pro-life regulations end up accelerating the industry's pivot to earlier, self-managed abortions.
questions.quote: As Sietstra pointed out, pro-life laws literally create a right to abortion as long as the murder is accomplished without the help of a third party. What she advocates is in accordance with the legal strategy of the pro-life movement. The abortion industry is quickly moving toward DIY abortions meaning that *the real accomplishment of pro-life regulations is expediting the evolution of the abortion industry toward earlier, self-managed abortions.*

questions.question: Hasn't abortion been curtailed since Roe was overturned?
questions.audience: Post-Roe policy analyst studying self-managed abortion
questions.answer: The article concedes the data is incomplete but argues self-managed abortion has surged since Dobbs, fueled by mail-order pill services like Aid Access and Women on Web and by hotlines that help mothers avoid the law. The bottom line in the piece is that abortion has hardly been curtailed and may now be easier to obtain.
questions.quote: After the overturn of *Roe Vs Wade,* self-managed abortion has absolutely skyrocketed, and this has been [shown in the data. (Click here)](https://www.axios.com/2022/11/01/abortion-pills-roe-reversal-study) While we don't have the complete picture of the data regarding self-managed abortion, what we can know for sure is that abortion has hardly been curtailed by the overturn of Roe, and in fact may now be easier to procure.

questions.question: Won't criminalizing abortion just lead to mass prosecution of women?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian reconsidering automatic immunity for mothers
questions.answer: The article argues the opposite: removing automatic immunity is meant to deter abortion before it happens, not to fill prisons with mothers. Just as theft penalties deter would-be thieves, the threat of homicide charges would tip the close-call decisions most women describe and prevent the killing in the first place.
questions.quote: Removing immunity for mothers who choose abortion doesn't mean we'll charge a million mothers and fathers who made excruciating decisions with murder. It means the vast majority will never murder their children in the first place. Penalties are a deterrent.

questions.question: Are there real testimonies that legal immunity influenced a mother's decision to abort?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian reconsidering automatic immunity for mothers
questions.answer: Yes. The article quotes abolitionist Cheryl Cali, who said at the 2020 Abolition Now Conference that she would not have walked into a Planned Parenthood at fifteen and killed her child if she had not been exempt from prosecution. Jill Robbins is cited echoing the same point.
questions.quote: At the 2020 Abolition Now Conference, abolitionist Cheryl Cali told the audience that her child would be with her today had equal justice been provided to preborn children at the time of her abortion: "If I were not exempt from being prosecuted for murdering my own child, I would not have gone into a Planned Parenthood at 15 years old and killed my baby."

questions.question: How did parental immunity in pre-Roe abortion bans actually help Roe v. Wade succeed?
questions.audience: State legislator weighing an equal protection bill
questions.answer: Justice Blackmun pointed out in footnote 54 of Roe that Texas treated the woman as neither a principal nor an accomplice, and that the penalty for criminal abortion was far below the penalty for murder. He used these inconsistencies to argue Texas itself didn't really treat the fetus as a person, undercutting the state's 14th Amendment defense. The article warns that returning to the same kind of immunity-based law repeats the very mistake Blackmun exploited.
questions.quote: "There are other inconsistencies between Fourteenth Amendment status and the typical abortion statute. It has already been pointed out that in Texas the woman is not a principal or an accomplice with respect to an abortion upon her. If the fetus is a person, why is the woman not a principal or an accomplice? Further, the penalty for criminal abortion specified by Art. 1195 is significantly less than the maximum penalty for murder prescribed by Art. 1257 of the Texas Penal Code. If the fetus is a person, may the penalties be different?"

questions.question: What concretely do pro-life laws criminalize if mothers are exempt?
questions.audience: Skeptic asking how abolitionists differ from the pro-life establishment
questions.answer: According to the article, immunity laws don't actually criminalize the killing of a preborn human being at all. They only criminalize third parties who help the mother, leaving self-managed abortion lawful. The abolitionist demand is the same legal protection for preborn humans that everyone else receives.
questions.quote: Pro-life laws that provide immunity to the mother do nothing to criminalize the act of murdering a preborn human being. All they do is criminalize the act of helping a mother murder her preborn son or daughter, and allow for self-managed abortion to continue.

related.title: A Mother Is a Magistrate: Why Duress Is No Defense for Abortion
related.reason: Directly extends the criminal-liability argument by rejecting duress carve-outs in equal protection bills.

related.title: Abolitionists Must Stand Firm to Oppose Murder, Love Murderers
related.reason: Answers the common pro-life claim that mothers shouldn't be punished, paired with how to love post-abortive women.

related.title: No Exceptions
related.reason: Companion AR pillar page on rejecting exceptions in abolition legislation.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Lays out the broader contrast with the pro-life movement that this page critiques on immunity.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual
related.reason: Pillar page on rejecting incrementalism, which is the strategy that produced the immunity rule.

related.title: Letter to Oklahoma Senators From a Post-Abortive Mother
related.reason: Post-abortive mother arguing for equal protection and criminalization, reinforcing the Cheryl Cali testimony in this piece.

related.reason: Detailed reporting on the Louisiana 2022 vote that this article uses as its central case study.
